free_text: Evolution of a spallation reaction: experiment and
Monte-Carlo simulation.
Corrections of some misprintings.
NESSI-detector=[NEutron Scintillator
tank and SIlicon ball]. The NESSI-detector consists of
two 4Pi detectors, the Berlin Neutron Ball (BNB) for
neutron detection and the Berlin Silicon Ball (BSiB)
for detection of all charged particles. BNB is a
spherical shell of liquid scintillator with an inner
and outer diameter of 40 cm and 140 cm, respectively.
The silicon ball BSiB is centered around the target in
the inner vacuum chamber (40 cm diameter) of BNB. BSiB
consists of 162 silicon detectors (500/zm thick)
arranged to a sphere with a diameter of 20 cm.
COSY Accelerator.
BNB description
BSiB description
-  By  Author - The experimental cross-sections for the
Z=1 particles are strongly influenced by the upper
energy  cut-off.  The silicon detectors of the BSiB are
able to measure the light charged particles within  the
following energy ranges:
P:  E-min = 2.2 MeV    E-max = 26 MeV
D:  E-min = 2.2 MeV    E-max = 49 MeV
T:  E-min = 2.2 MeV    E-max = 76 MeV
He:  E-min = 2.2 MeV    E-max = 120 MeV
Only a small portion of D and T is lost by the upper
cut-off energy and the portion of He with energies
larger  than  120  MeV  is  completely  negligible.  In
contrast to this, the upper threshold for the protons
is essential due to the suppression of the high
energetic protons from the inc-cascade.  For the larger
targets the losses caused by the lower threshold are
very small, but are increasing for lighter targets in
particular for hydrogen.  Therefore it is important for
all the given hydrogen cross-section to relate them to
the experimental covered energy range.
Comparison of experimental and calculated
excitation energy distributions with the condition of
at least one evaporation-like detected charged particle
for the reaction 1.2 GeV p+Fe and 1.8 GeV p+Au.

- By Authors -
1.  The Total time resolution was about 0.9 nanoseconds
resulting in better than unit mass resolution for
stopped particles with A<4 and about 20 mass units for
2. Mass yields for mass number >4 are not corrected for
detector efficiency, which is decreasing for increasing
masses.
The particle mass was deduced from the measured time of
flight and energy corrected for plasma delay and pulse
height defect, respectively.
The datum was corrected in C.-M.Herbach's letter.

- By Authors - Here the upper  energy  cut-off  is  the
same as listed in O0678001 but the lower thresholds are
the following
P: E-min = 2.8 MeV
D: E-min = 3.6 MeV
T: E-min = 4.1 MeV
He-3: E-min = 9.7 MeV
He-4: E-min = 10.8 MeV
Most important once is the upper cut-of for protons  at
26 MeV with a strong influence on the experimental
cross-section.  The other thresholds are less
important.

data are gated by neutron multiplicity
Low limit of measured neutron multiplicity.
High limit of measured neutron multiplicity.
- By Authors - The Energy spectra of helium which are
shown for neutron multiplicities are empty within the
Range 31-35 MeV.  This is due to the problems which
occur when the kinetic energies are determined closed
to the punch through point of the helium particles [at
about 31 MeV for 500 mu-m Si-detectors].  Within
this region it is not clear whether the helium has been
stopped in the detector or not.  Therefore we decide
not to show the yield for this energy range.
SD: REACTION code was corrected. Sum of two
reactions was written as one. Data = 0 were deleted.
Six BSiB detectors were replaced
by  telescopes consisting of two fully depleted delta-E
silicon detectors backed by a 7 cm thick CsI
scintillator with photo-diode read-out.
-  By  Authors  -  Here the upper energy cut-off is the
same as listed in O0678001 but the lower threshold is
changed.
Most  important once is the upper cut-off for protons
at 26 MeV with a strong influence on the experimental
cross-section.  The other thresholds are less
important.
No any analysis is presented.
